goal.value: Implement auto-repoint PRs after bottom-of-chain merge. Branch: feat/repoint-after-merge off feat/pr-body. 3 phases: (1) metadata propagation in sync, (2) repoint detection in push, (3) ez sync --submit. Also: no_repoint config, fix bulk PR check, --repoint flag.

progress.value:
Phase 1-3: ALL IMPLEMENTED, 287/287 tests pass
Commits: cf739d5, 88571cc, 947f9ed (+ uncommitted fix in push.rs)

CURRENT TASK: Fix repoint logic in push.rs, then real-world test
Uncommitted changes in worktree src/cmd/push.rs — partially rewritten repoint block

KEY FIX NEEDED in push.rs create arm (around line 435):
- After repoint close+create succeeds, ALWAYS persist pr_repo even without --repo
- Current code: `if let Some(ref r) = resolved_override { pr_repo = Some(r) }`
- Need: `if did_repoint || resolved_override.is_some() { pr_repo = Some(effective_repo) }`
- `did_repoint` bool is already in the tuple from repoint detection block

ALSO: The repoint block now does NOT mutate state on close — only create path sets state.
The `did_repoint` flag tracks whether we closed an old PR and are creating fresh.

REAL-WORLD TEST PLAN:
1. Build+install binary
2. On test/repoint-copy branch (currently at dezren39/ez-stack PR#6, OPEN)
3. `ez push --repoint --repo developing-today-forks/ez-stack` → close #6, create at dtf
4. `ez push --repoint --repo dezren39/ez-stack` → close dtf PR, recreate at fork
5. Close final PR, delete test/repoint-copy branch
6. Commit the fix, amend or new commit
